commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

commit.message:
chore: restructure eval suites — AP product QA + benchmarks split (#686)

## What

Replaces generic capability evals with AP-specific product QA suite.
Separates external benchmarks.

### New: `evals/suites/ap-qa/` (146 tasks)
| File | Category | Tasks |
|---|---|---|
| `tools.yaml` | Tool verification (console, exec, web_search,
knowledge, memory, subscriptions, topics, delegation, messaging, timers,
self-update) | 62 |
| `behavior.yaml` | T1 personality (sycophancy resistance, opinions,
communication style, identity) + delegation behavior (triggers, T2
planning, T3 execution, round trip) | 43 |
| `safety.yaml` | Budget gates, approval gates, security hardening,
taint system | 20 |
| `scenarios.yaml` | Multi-turn conversations, error recovery,
concurrent work, real e2e tasks, edge cases | 21 |

### Replaced (deleted)
- `core.yaml` (16 tasks) — generic math/reasoning → absorbed into tools
+ behavior
- `extended.yaml` (20 tasks) — generic reasoning/safety → absorbed into
behavior + safety
- `terminal-bench.yaml` (10 tasks) — generic tool-use → absorbed into
tools
- `subscriptions.yaml` (14 tasks) — absorbed into tools

### Moved to `evals/benchmarks/`
- `gaia-sample.yaml`, `humaneval-sample.yaml`, `swe-bench-sample.yaml`

### Kept
- `real-issues.yaml` — tests T2 planning quality on real AP architecture
issues

### Also
- Updated grid configs to reference new suite paths
- Updated README with new structure + run commands

## Why
Old evals tested 'is the LLM smart?' (math, syllogisms). New evals test
'does AP work as intended?' — tools, personality, delegation, safety,
real scenarios. Product QA, not benchmarking.

commit.message:
fix(e2e): F1e modal focus trap — wait for React hydration + explicit key sequence

Root cause: waitForReviewPageReady only checks h1 visibility (SSR output),
but keyboard bindings are registered via useEffect which runs AFTER hydration.
The Ctrl+Shift+/ keypress fires before bindings exist.

Fix:
- Add waitForReviewPageHydrated() that waits for finding grid rows (confirms
  React effects including keyboard binding registration have executed)
- Click body to ensure headless Chromium has keyboard focus on the page
- Use explicit keyboard.down/up sequence for cross-platform reliability
- Increase dialog visibility timeout to 10s

commit.message:
Fix CX ticket visibility mismatch and back-navigation bug

Problem 1 — 403 on ticket open (Tooba/Hamza):
filterTicketsByDepartmentAccess used isCXTicketVisible() which matched
ANY ticket with a vendorHandle, regardless of department. So CX users
saw Finance/Ops/etc tickets in their list (75 tickets from 4 depts)
but canViewTicket gates by ticket.department === 'CX', causing 403
when they clicked any non-CX ticket.

Fix: filterTicketsByDepartmentAccess for CX now uses
ticket.department === 'CX' — consistent with canViewTicket.
CX users see only CX tickets (+ tickets assigned to them).

Problem 2 — Back button always goes to All Tickets:
When a ticket returned 403/404, the 'Ticket not found' screen had a
hardcoded setLocation('/tickets') button, ignoring the ?from= param.
So Tooba opening a ticket from My Tickets and hitting 403 would
land on All Tickets instead of returning to My Tickets.

Fix: 'Back to Tickets' now calls getBackPath() which respects the
?from=my-tickets / ?from=all-tickets query parameter.

commit.message:
state, stagedsync: 2-cache IBS refactor baseline tests (Phase 1) (#19708)

Closes #19700 — Phase 1 of #19623 (Rationalize IntraBlockState to a
2-Cache Model).

## Summary

Tests only — no production code changes. Establishes the invariant
baseline before the 5-phase refactoring of `IntraBlockState` to a
2-cache model.

### `execution/state/ibs_2cache_test.go`

| Test | What it proves |
|---|---|
| `TestVersionedWritesMatchStateObjects` | Every stateObject field
(balance, nonce, code, storage) is reflected in `VersionedWrites` with
the same value. This is the invariant Phase 2 relies on to eliminate the
IBS round-trip. |
| `TestSnapshotRandomWithVersionMap` | Snapshot/revert with versionMap
leaves both stateObject accessors and `VersionedWrites` consistent —
reverts work correctly in both caches simultaneously. |
| `TestCommittedStateWithVersionMap` | `GetCommittedState` returns the
pre-tx value (EIP-1283 original value) when reading through versionMap
at `txIndex-1`. Required for correct SSTORE gas calculation. |
| `TestStateUpdatesEquivalence` | **Skipped** — placeholder for Phase 2
(#19701), which implements `StateUpdatesFromVersionedWrites`. |
| `TestCrossBlockStateReadConsistency` | Block N writes via
`FinalizeTx`+`Writer`; a fresh block N+1 IBS reads the correct values
from `SharedDomains`. Baseline for Phase 3. |
| `TestDomainApplyFromVersionedWrites` | `VersionedWrites` applied
through the existing round-trip (`ApplyVersionedWrites → IBS →
FinalizeTx → Writer`) produce correct domain state. Phase 2 must
reproduce this without the round-trip. |

### `execution/stagedsync/exec3_2cache_test.go`

| Test | What it proves |
|---|---|
| `TestCrossBlockTimingRace` | `rs.accounts`
(`StateV3Buffered.accounts`) bridges the timing hole: block N+1 workers
read block N's state via `NewBufferedReader` even before the async
`applyResults` goroutine has updated `SharedDomains`. Passes with
current code. Phase 3 (#19702) will make it pass via synchronous domain
apply instead, then remove `rs.accounts`. |

## Test plan

- [x] All new tests pass (`go test ./execution/state/...
./execution/stagedsync/...`)
- [x] `TestStateUpdatesEquivalence` correctly skips
- [x] Existing tests unaffected (`make test-short`)
